meals.name: Sausage White Bean Soup
meals.label: Meal A
ings: 1 can (15 oz) white beans, drained and rinsed
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add crumbled sausage and cook until browned, breaking it apart as it cooks. Remove sausage and set aside, leaving the fat in the pot.
Add onion, garlic, carrots, and celery to the same pot. Cook until softened, about 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Return sausage to pot. Add white beans, chicken broth, thyme, rosemary, salt, and pepper. Stir well to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 20–25 minutes until vegetables are tender and flavors have melded.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh parsley. Serve with crusty bread if desired.
meals.note: 💡 Mash a handful of the white beans against the side of the pot before serving — it makes the broth wonderfully creamy without any cream. Use mild Italian sausage to keep it kid-friendly.

meals.name: Air Fryer Fish Tacos with Cilantro Lime Slaw
meals.label: Meal B
ings: 1½ lb tilapia or white fish (cod, mahi mahi)
¼ tsp sea salt, ¼ tsp cracked pepper
Small flour or corn tortillas for serving
steps: Mix together all spice rub ingredients (chili powder, oregano, garlic powder, paprika, onion powder, cumin, salt, pepper — skip cayenne). Set aside.
Make the cilantro lime slaw: toss coleslaw mix with mayo, lime juice, minced garlic, cilantro, sugar, salt, and pepper. Refrigerate while fish cooks.
Spray air fryer basket with oil. Pat fish dry and lay in basket. Brush with a little olive oil. Coat generously with the spice rub and press gently into the fish. Spritz with oil.
Preheat air fryer to 400°F. Cook fish for 8–10 minutes until opaque and easily flakes. Internal temp should reach 145°F. Do not flip — fish may fall apart.
Remove fish from air fryer. Cut or flake into pieces. Warm tortillas. Assemble tacos: fish + slaw + squeeze of fresh lime. Serve immediately.
meals.note: 💡 Don't flip the fish in the air fryer — it will fall apart. No oven? Bake at 400°F for about 15 minutes instead. Slaw can be made 30 min ahead and chilled.

meals.name: Miso Glazed Salmon Bowls
meals.label: Meal C
ings: 1 teaspoon fresh ginger, grated (or ½ tsp ground)
1 large English cucumber, thinly sliced or diced
steps: Cook rice: combine 1½ cups jasmine rice + 3 cups water in a pot. Bring to a boil, reduce to low, cover and cook 18 minutes. Fluff with a fork.
Make miso glaze: whisk together miso paste, soy sauce, honey, rice vinegar, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic until smooth.
Preheat oven to 400°F (or use broiler on high). Line a baking sheet with foil. Place salmon fillets on the sheet and spoon miso glaze generously over each fillet.
Bake 12–15 minutes until salmon flakes easily. For extra caramelization, broil for the last 2–3 minutes — watch closely so the glaze doesn't burn.
Build bowls: start with rice, then add salmon fillet on top. Arrange edamame, cucumber, and shredded carrots around it. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
Adults can drizzle with extra soy sauce or a little sriracha. Serve immediately.
meals.note: 💡 The miso glaze gets sticky and lacquered under the broiler — that's the goal. The cucumber and carrots add great crunch against the rich salmon. Totally kid-friendly as written.
